topic.what: Attack vectors against FIDO2/passkey authentication systems
topic.how: Reviews prior attacks on passkeys, implements two novel attack demonstrations (Infected Authenticator using KeePassXC, Authenticator Deception via DNS/ARP spoofing), classifies attack complexity and resource requirements
topic.why_matters: Evaluates whether FIDO2 authentically provides phishing-resistant authentication as marketed; determines real-world security posture versus traditional passwords

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of successful attack execution given all prerequisites met
characteristics.evidence: Both experiments (Infected Authenticator targeting Google via KeePassXC, Authenticator Deception targeting linear.app) completed full attack flow successfully
characteristics.context: Attacks succeeded but required substantial prerequisites (malware installation, device compromise, physical proximity)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Difficulty level required to execute each attack vector
characteristics.evidence: Passkey Reduction (easy), Bluetooth AITM (medium), Authenticator Deception (medium-hard), Infected Authenticator (medium-hard), Victim Impersonation via AITM (hard)
characteristics.context: All attacks require substantial effort; none offer remote exploitation without device compromise

characteristics.what_it_measures: Prerequisites and resources required for successful attack
characteristics.evidence: All attacks require: malware installation on victim device, physical proximity (ARP spoofing), device compromise, or AITM positioning; no remote attacks demonstrated
characteristics.context: Key finding: no method exists for remotely exploiting FIDO2 without first compromising victim system

characteristics.what_it_measures: Degree to which FIDO2 prevents traditional phishing attacks
characteristics.evidence: Private key never leaves authenticator; passkeys bound to domain; conventional phishing techniques ineffective
characteristics.context: Core claim validated: passkeys cannot be phished using conventional techniques

characteristics.what_it_measures: Relative security improvement of FIDO2 over password-based auth
characteristics.evidence: Attacks require substantial effort, malware, physical proximity vs simple credential phishing for passwords
characteristics.context: FIDO2 substantially raises attacker cost and complexity compared to password systems
